title: Nanopore amplicon sequencing
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4eeu3","text":"This protocol describes the step-by-step procedures for performing nanopore amplicon sequencing using the latest Kit 14 chemistry. The method is versatile and can be applied to a wide range of sequence-based analyses, including human microbiome profiling and biodiversity assessment using environmental DNA (eDNA metabarcoding).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: In the first-round PCR reaction, the region of interest is amplified using specific primers flanked by anchor sequences. The tailed PCR products are then subjected to a second round of PCR to incorporate barcode sequences for multiplexing. The barcoded amplicons are pooled and end-prepped, allowing for the ligation of sequencing adapters.

Prepare the PCR master mix. A B C 1 Component Volume Final conc. 2 Template DNA x µL 3 5 µM FW/RV primer mix 1.5–2.5 µL 0.3–0.5 µM each 4 PrimeSTAR Max Ver.2 Premix (2X) 12.5 µL 1X 5 Water up to 25 µL PrimeSTAR Max DNA Polymerase Ver.2 Takara Bio Inc. Catalog #R047A Tailed primers (user-supplied) A B 1 Primer Sequence 2 Forward (FW) 5'-TTTCTGTTGGTGCTGATATTGC - target-specific sequence -3' 3 Reverse (RV) 5'-ACTTGCCTGTCGCTCTATCTTC - target-specific sequence -3' The 5' anchor sequences serve as priming sites for barcoded primers used in the 2nd PCR. The following tailed primers are used for amplifying the V1–V9 region of the bacterial 16S rRNA gene. 16S rRNA gene-specific sequences are in bold letters. 27F * : 5'-TTTCTGTTGGTGCTGATATTGC AGRGTTYGATYMTGGCTCAG -3' 1492R: 5'-ACTTGCCTGTCGCTCTATCTTC CRGHTACCTTGTTACGACTT -3' * We observed that the 27F universal primer described in Protocol Version 5 exhibits reduced amplification efficiency and specificity, likely due to its increased degeneracy. To avoid these issues, the primer used in the previous version of the protocol, as described above, is recommended.

Perform PCR. A B C D 1 Step Temperature Time Cycles 2 Denaturation 98°C 10 sec 25–35 3 Annealing 57°C 15 sec 4 Extension 68°C 5–10 sec/kb* 5 Hold 4°C ∞ 1 * e.g. Use an extension time of 10 seconds per cycle to amplify the near-full length (V1–V9 regions) of bacterial 16S rRNA genes (approximately 1.5 kbp), using the high-speed PrimeSTAR DNA polymerase.
Analyze 1–2 µL of the PCR products by gel electrophoresis to verify successful amplification.

Prepare the PCR master mix. A B 1 Component Volume 2 First-round PCR products 1.0 µL 3 PCR Barcode (BC)* 0.5 µL 4 PrimeSTAR Max Ver.2 Premix (2X) 12.5 µL 5 Water 11 µL 6 Total 25 µL * One of barcoded primers (10 µM BC) supplied in the PCR Barcoding Expansion (EXP-PBC001 or EXP-PBC096). PCR Barcoding Expansion 1–12 Oxford Nanopore Technologies Catalog #EXP-PBC001 PCR Barcoding Expansion 1–96 Oxford Nanopore Technologies Catalog #EXP-PBC096
Allow the ProNex beads to equilibrate to room temperature.

Add ProNex beads to the pooled sample and mix by pipetting. A B 1 Component Volume 2 Pooled sample 100 µL 3 ProNex Chemistry 100 µL This is an example protocol based on an input sample volume of 100 µL. The ratio of ProNex beads to sample varies depending on the desired DNA size cutoff range. The condition shown above will enrich DNA fragments larger than 1 kbp using a 1:1 ratio of ProNex beads to sample. As an alternative to ProNex beads, AMPure XP can also be used. The ratio of AMPure beads to the sample should be adjusted accordingly.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Place the tube on a magnetic rack for 00:02:00 .
Pipette off the supernatant.
Wash the beads with 80% ethanol as follows:
Spin down the tube and place it back on the magnetic rack.
Pipette off any residual ethanol.
Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and allow the sample to air-dry for ~2 min.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Place the tube on the magnetic rack for 00:02:00 .
Transfer the eluate to a new tube.
Warm QuantiFluor ONE dsDNA dye to Room temperature . QuantiFluor ONE dsDNA System Promega Catalog #E4871
Add 1 µL of eluted sample to 200 µL of QuantiFluor ONE dsDNA dye in 0.5 mL tube.
Mix thoroughly by vortexing.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 , protected from light.
Measure fluorescence using the Quantus Fluorometer to quantify DNA concentration. Quantus Fluorometer (Promega, E6150)
Open the MinION lid and insert a flow cell (R10.4.1) under the clip. MinION Flow Cell (R10.4.1) (Oxford Nanopore Technologies, FLO-MIN114)
Perform flow cell check.
Check the number of active pores available for the experiment.

Prepare the flow cell priming mix. A B 1 Component Volume 2 Flow Cell Tether (FCT) 30 µL 3 50 mg/mL Bovine Serum Albumin (BSA) 5 µL 4 Flow Cell Flush (FCF) 1,170 µL 5 Total 1,205 µL The Flow Cell Tether (FCT) and Flow Cell Flush (FCF) are included in the Ligation Sequencing Kit V14. UltraPure BSA (50 mg/mL) Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #AM2616
Load the priming mix into the flow cell through the priming port as follows: Avoid introducing air.
Gently mix the sequencing library by pipetting just prior to loading.

Load 75 µL of the sequencing library into the flow cell via the SpotON sample port in a dropwise fashion. Using a P100 or P200 micropipette, let each drop flow into the sample port before adding the next one.

Set up a sequencing run on the MinKNOW software. A B 1 Parameter Setting 2 Flow cell type FLO-MIN114 (R10.4.1) 3 Kit Ligation Sequencing Kit SQK-LSK114 4 Expansion pack PCR Barcoding Expansion 1–12 EXP-PBC001 5 Basecalling On 6 Basecalling model Fast basecalling 7 Barcoding On 8 Trim barcodes On 9 Barcode both ends On 10 Q score filtering 8 (default value) 11 Min/Max read length* Optional Above are typical examples of run parameters for real-time basecalling on the MinION Mk1D, connected to an Apple Silicon Mac. * For full-length 16S rRNA gene sequencing, we set the minimum read length to 1,300 bases and the maximum read length to 1,800 bases for a passing read.
Repeat steps 27.1 and 27.2 for a total of two washes.
Keeping on the magnetic rack, add 200 µL of 80% ethanol without disturbing the bead pellet.
Discard the supernatant.
Open the priming port cover of the flow cell.
If any air bubbles are present under the cover, remove them using the following procedure:
Set the volume of P1000 micropipette to 200 µL.
Insert the pipette tip into the priming port.

Turn the wheel of the pipette slowly to increase the volume and draw back 20–30 µL of the buffer. Care must be taken not to remove too much, ensuring that the sensor array of the flow cell remains covered by the buffer.
Wait for 00:05:00 .
Gently lower the SpotON sample port cover back, ensuring the bung enters the sample port.
Stop the sequencing run.

Prepare the flow cell wash mix and gently mix by pipetting. A B 1 Component Volume 2 Wash Mix (WMX)* 2 µL 3 Wash Diluent (DIL) 398 µL 4 Total 400 µL * WMX contains a nuclease, DNase I, which digests the DNA library. Flow Cell Wash Kit Oxford Nanopore Technologies Catalog #EXP-WSH004
Using a P1000 micropipette, aspirate the fluid from the waste channel through the waste port. Ensure that both the priming port and SpotON sample port are closed.
Open the priming port cover of the flow cell.
[Optional] If necessary, remove air bubbles under the cover by following the procedure in step 86.
Load the wash mix into the flow cell via the priming port as follows: Avoid introducing air.
Close the priming port.
Wait for 01:00:00 to digest the residual DNA on the flow cell.
Open the priming port cover of the flow cell.
[Optional] If necessary, remove air bubbles under the cover by following the procedure in step 86.
Using a P1000 micropipette, load 500 µL of Storage Buffer (S) into the flow cell via the priming port by slowly turning the pipette wheel down. Avoid introducing air.
Close the priming port.
Store the flow cell at 4 °C for subsequent use.
Using a P1000 micropipette, aspirate the fluid from the waste channel through the waste port. Ensure that both the priming port and SpotON sample port are closed.

Pool the barcoded amplicons into a single tube. As an example, when working with 10 barcodes, pool 10 µL from each sample to obtain a final volume of 100 µL; alternatively, the samples can be pooled in desired ratios, and the total volume can be adjusted flexibly depending on the experimental requirements. Each sample may also be cleaned up separately prior to pooling. After quantifying the DNA, the samples can be pooled in the desired ratios to balance input amounts and achieve uniform sequencing data, and subsequently subjected to the End-prep (step 41).
Resuspend the beads in 26 µL of water.

Mix the following components. A B 1 Component Volume 2 Pooled amplicon DNA 25 µL 3 NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Reaction Buffer 3.5 µL 4 NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Enzyme Mix 1.5 µL 5 Total 30 µL NEBNext Ultra II End Repair/dA-Tailing Module - 24 rxns New England Biolabs Catalog #E7546S
Prepare 200 fmol of pooled DNA (from step 35) in 25 µL of water. For full-length 16S rRNA gene sequencing, 200 fmol of 1.5 kbp amplicons correspond to approximately 200 ng in weight.

Incubate the reaction using a thermal cycler. A B 1 Temperature Time 2 20°C 5 min 3 65°C 5 min 4 4°C ∞ Amplicons are converted into 5ʹ-phosphorylated and 3ʹ-dA-tailed DNA, enabling ligation of Nanopore sequencing adapters.
Allow the ProNex beads to equilibrate to room temperature.

Add ProNex beads to the end-prep reaction and mix by pipetting. A B 1 Component Volume 2 End-prepped DNA 30 µL 3 ProNex Chemistry 30 µL The ratio of beads to sample volume should be adjusted according to the desired DNA fragment size. As an alternative to ProNex beads, AMPure XP can also be used.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Place the tube on a magnetic rack for 00:02:00 .
Pipette off the supernatant.
Wash the beads with 80% ethanol as follows:
Repeat steps 49.1 and 49.2 for a total of two washes.
Keeping on the magnetic rack, add 200 µL of 80% ethanol without disturbing the bead pellet.
Discard the supernatant.
Spin down the tube and place it back on the magnetic rack.
Pipette off any residual ethanol.
Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and allow the sample to air-dry for ~2 min.
Resuspend the beads in 30 µL of water.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Place the tube on the magnetic rack for 00:02:00 .
Transfer the eluate to a new tube.

Mix the following components. A B 1 Component Volume 2 End-prepped DNA 30 µL 3 Ligation Buffer (LNB)* 12.5 µL 4 Ligation Adapter (LA) 2.5 µL 5 Salt-T4 DNA Ligase 5 µL 6 Total 50 µL The Ligation Buffer (LNB) and Ligation Adapter (LA) are included in the Ligation Sequencing Kit V14. * LNB is viscous and should be thoroughly mixed by pipetting. Ligation Sequencing Kit V14 Oxford Nanopore Technologies Catalog #SQK-LSK114 Salt-T4 DNA Ligase New England Biolabs Catalog #M0467S
Allow the ProNex beads to equilibrate to room temperature.

Add ProNex beads to the adapter-ligated sample and mix by pipetting. A B 1 Component Volume 2 Adapter-ligated DNA 50 µL 3 ProNex Chemistry 50 µL The ratio of beads to sample volume should be adjusted according to the desired DNA fragment size. As an alternative to ProNex beads, AMPure XP can also be used.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Place the tube on a magnetic rack for 00:02:00 .
Pipette off the supernatant.

Wash the beads with either Short Fragment Buffer (SFB) or Long Fragment Buffer (LFB) as follows: The Short Fragment Buffer (SFB) and Long Fragment Buffer (LFB) are included in the Ligation Sequencing Kit V14. For full-length 16S rRNA gene amplicons (approximately 1.5 kbp), use SFB to retain DNA fragments of all sizes. LFB should be used to enrich for DNA fragments larger than 3 kbp.
Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and resuspend the beads in 100 µL of either SFB or LFB.
Discard the supernatant.
Repeat steps 65.1–65.3 for a total of two washes.
Spin down the tube and place it back on the magnetic rack.
Pipette off any residual wash buffer.
Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and allow the sample to air-dry for ~2 min.
Resuspend the beads in 15 µL of Elution Buffer (EB). The Elution Buffer (EB) is included in the Ligation Sequencing Kit V14.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Place the tube on the magnetic rack for 00:02:00 .
Transfer the eluate to a new tube.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:10:00 .
Place the tube on the magnetic rack for 00:02:00 .
Warm QuantiFluor ONE dsDNA dye to Room temperature .
Add 1 µL of eluted sample to 200 µL of QuantiFluor ONE dsDNA dye in 0.5 mL tube.
Mix thoroughly by vortexing.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 , protected from light.
Measure fluorescence using the Quantus Fluorometer to quantify DNA concentration.
Using a P1000 micropipette, aspirate 800 µL of the priming mix.
Insert the pipette tip into the priming port.
Slowly turn the pipette wheel down to load the priming mix into the flow cell.

For 1–10 kbp amplicons, prepare 35–50 fmol of DNA library (from step 73) in 12 µL of Elution Buffer (EB). For full-length 16S rRNA gene sequencing, 35–50 fmol of 1.5 kbp amplicons correspond to approximately 35–50 ng in weight.

Prepare the sequencing library for loading. A B 1 Component Volume 2 DNA library 12 µL 3 Sequencing Buffer (SB) 37.5 µL 4 Library Beads (LIB)* 25.5 µL 5 Total 75 µL The Sequencing Buffer (SB) and Library Beads (LIB) are included in the Ligation Sequencing Kit V14. * Since LIB settle quickly, the bead suspension should be mixed immediately before adding it to the loading mixture.
Lift the SpotON sample port cover upwards to uncover the sample port on the flow cell.

Load the remaining 200 µL of the priming mix into the priming port (caution: not the SpotON sample port) by slowly turning the pipette wheel down to dispense the mix and complete the flow cell priming.
Place the light shield onto the flow cell.
Connect the MinION sequencer to the host computer and start the MinKNOW software. MinION Mk1D (Sequencer, Oxford Nanopore Technologies, MIN-101D)
Start a sequencing run.
Remove the flow cell from the MinION device and return it to the tray.
Insert the flow cell into the MinION device.
Close the MinION lid.
Remove the flow cell from the MinION device and place it in the tray.
Using a P1000 micropipette, aspirate 200 µL of the wash mix.
Insert the pipette tip into the priming port.
Slowly turn the pipette wheel down to load the wash mix into the flow cell.
Wait for 00:05:00 .
Load the remaining 200 µL of the wash mix into the priming port by following the procedure in steps 108.1–108.3.
Close the priming port

Allow the ProNex beads to equilibrate to room temperature. The first-round PCR products may need to be purified before proceeding to the second-round of PCR. This additional step removes reaction contaminants, which would be beneficial for the downstream analysis. ProNex Size-Selective Purification System Promega Catalog #NG2001

Add ProNex beads to the first-round PCR products and mix by pipetting. %s %s The ratio of ProNex beads to sample varies depending on the desired DNA size cutoff range. The condition shown above will enrich DNA fragments larger than 1 kbp using a 1:1 ratio of ProNex beads to sample. As an alternative to ProNex beads, AMPure XP can also be used. The ratio of AMPure beads to the sample should be adjusted accordingly.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Place the tube on a magnetic rack for 00:02:00 . NGS MagnaStand v.3 8Ch (Magnetic rack (0.2 mL tube), FastGene, FG-SSMAG3)
Pipette off the supernatant.
Wash the beads once with 80% ethanol using the following procedure:
Keeping on the magnetic rack, add 200 µL of 80% ethanol without disturbing the bead pellet.
Discard the supernatant.
Spin down the tube and place it back on the magnetic rack.
Pipette off any residual ethanol.
Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and allow the sample to air-dry for ~2 min.
Resuspend the beads in 20 µL of water.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Place the tube on the magnetic rack for 00:02:00 .
Transfer the eluate to a new tube.

Perform PCR to incorporate barcode sequences. A B C D 1 Step Temperature Time Cycles 2 Denaturation 98°C 10 sec 5–10 3 Annealing 57°C 15 sec 4 Extension 68°C 5–10 sec/kb* 5 Hold 4°C ∞ 1 *e.g. Use an extension time of 10 seconds per cycle to amplify the near-full length (V1–V9 regions) of bacterial 16S rRNA genes (approximately 1.5 kbp), using the high-speed PrimeSTAR DNA polymerase.
Analyze 1–2 µL of the PCR products by gel electrophoresis.
creator: Yoshiyuki Matsuo
